# Research Output Templates

Project-agnostic templates for the Researcher agent. Each section contains a ready-to-use markdown template (as shown) and a brief "When to use" note.

---

## 1. Quick Summary Template
For rapid topic overviews (300-500 words).

```markdown
# <Topic> — Quick Summary
Date: YYYY-MM-DD
Sources: <count> papers/articles reviewed

## TL;DR
<2-3 sentence summary>

## Key Points
- ...

## Notable Sources
- [Title](url) — <one-line takeaway>

## Confidence: <low/medium/high>
<Brief justification>
```

When to use: Fast briefings, internal memos, or to give stakeholders a concise orientation.

---

## 2. Literature Review Template
For comprehensive reviews of a research area (~1000-2000 words).

```markdown
# Literature Review: <Topic>
Date: YYYY-MM-DD | Sources reviewed: <N>

## Background & Motivation
...

## Methodology
<How papers were selected, search terms, databases used>

## Thematic Analysis
### Theme 1: <Name>
...
### Theme 2: <Name>
...

## Gaps & Open Questions
...

## Conclusions & Recommendations
...

## References
1. ...
```

When to use: In-depth synthesis, preparing related-work sections, or to justify a new research direction. Include selection methodology for reproducibility.

---

## 3. Paper Analysis Template
For deep-dive analysis of a single paper.

```markdown
# Paper Analysis: <Title>
Authors: <names> | Year: <year> | Venue: <journal/conference>
DOI/URL: <link>

## Summary
<What was done, why, how>

## Key Contributions
- ...

## Methodology
<Approach, datasets, experimental setup>

## Results
<Main findings, quantitative results>

## Strengths
- ...

## Limitations
- ...

## Relevance & Impact
<Why this matters, citation context, relation to field>
```

When to use: Evaluate a paper's contributions, quality, and relevance for citation or inclusion in a review.

---

## 4. Comparison Table Template
For comparing methods, tools, approaches, or papers.

```markdown
# Comparison: <Topic>
Date: YYYY-MM-DD

## Criteria
<What dimensions are being compared>

| Criterion | Option A | Option B | Option C |
|-----------|----------|----------|----------|
| Approach  | ...      | ...      | ...      |
| Performance | ...    | ...      | ...      |
| Limitations | ...   | ...      | ...      |
| Maturity  | ...      | ...      | ...      |

## Analysis
<Discussion of trade-offs, recommendations>

## Verdict
<Recommendation with justification>
```

When to use: Side-by-side comparisons to support decisions (tool selection, algorithm choice). Prefer including quantitative metrics.

---

## 5. Research Briefing Template
For periodic updates on a watched topic.

```markdown
# Research Briefing: <Topic>
Period: <start> to <end> | Sources: <databases>

## Executive Summary
<3-5 bullet points>

## New Developments
### <Development 1>
...

## Trend Analysis
- ...

## Implications
- ...

## Recommended Reading
- [Title](url) — why it matters

## Next Steps
- ...
```

When to use: Scheduled updates (weekly/monthly/quarterly) to inform stakeholders about recent papers, products, or landscape shifts.

---

## 6. Hypothesis & Experiment Design Template
For proposing research directions and experiments.

```markdown
# Research Proposal: <Title>
Date: YYYY-MM-DD

## Hypothesis
<Clear, testable hypothesis>

## Background
<Why this matters, prior evidence>

## Proposed Methodology
- ...

## Expected Outcomes
- ...

## Risks & Mitigations
- ...

## Required Resources
- ...
```

When to use: Drafting experiments for internal review, collaboration, or funding. Make hypotheses and evaluation criteria explicit.

---

Notes:
- Keep outputs concise, evidence-focused, and include links and counts when possible.
- Templates are intentionally neutral so they can be reused across domains.

Verified: Created standardized templates for quick summaries, literature reviews, paper analyses, comparisons, briefings, and experimental designs. Adapt fields as needed.
